hinge: Subgingival overhanging margins create plaque traps in a low-oxygen niche that favors anaerobic gram-negative periodontal pathogens. Smooth subgingival margins are less retentive and supragingival overhangs sit above the sulcus.

Influenza spreads primarily by respiratory droplets, not true airborne transmission like TB. Decreasing direct and indirect contact plus droplet precautions (masks, hand hygiene, distancing) is the standard infection-control strategy, not HEPA filtration alone.

If the RPD rest seats perfectly on the cast but are short intraorally, the cast does not accurately represent oral anatomy. Remake the final impression and framework to correct the master cast error — redoing on the same faulty cast repeats the error.

Bonding near a recent surgical site fails mainly because of blood, saliva, and moisture contamination that prevents reliable adhesive polymerization. Poor isolation is the primary cause, not faulty materials or wrong wire selection alone.

Oral bilateral white striae with atrophy plus genital ulcers and wrist lesions describes lichen planus. Topical corticosteroid (triamcinolone acetonide) is first-line local therapy — antivirals and antifungals do not treat the underlying inflammatory condition.

When proximal margins are sealed and sound but the composite is undercontoured with an open contact, conservative repair — removing the defective proximal portion and rebuilding contour/contact — is preferred over full restoration replacement when the remainder is intact.
Positive venereal disease serology with an oral ulcer indicates syphilis. Penicillin is the drug of choice for Treponema pallidum. Acyclovir treats herpesviruses, not syphilis.

Approximately 25% bone loss with imperfect glycemic control (HbA1c ~8%) typically maps to a fair periodontal prognosis — worse than good but not automatically hopeless or questionable without other severe complicating factors.
Maxillary first premolar has a short root trunk and deep mesial developmental concavity, making furcation access and prognosis the worst among common teeth listed.

Paroxysmal hemicrania (and other indomethacin-responsive trigeminal autonomic cephalalgias) characteristically responds dramatically to indomethacin, which serves both diagnostic and therapeutic roles. Oxygen is the classic acute abortive therapy for cluster headache, not paroxysmal hemicrania.

When an asthmatic patient's own bronchodilator fails and breathing remains difficult, activate EMS immediately. Continue oxygen and rescue bronchodilator per emergency protocol — do not rely on repeated self-administered inhaler alone in a deteriorating situation.

Radiating left arm and jaw pain in an obese diabetic suggests acute coronary syndrome. Glyceryl trinitrate (nitroglycerin) is the classic anti-anginal agent given alongside aspirin when blood pressure allows and no contraindications exist.

Severe pain within ~24 hours after instrumentation of a necrotic tooth with apical pathology is a classic endodontic flare-up from extruded debris and inflammatory mediators, not necessarily a missed canal, new crack, or wrong tooth diagnosis.

For apical surgery on a molar with a large periapical lesion, an intrasulcular flap with vertical releasing incision provides wide access and good visibility of the surgical field. Semilunar flaps offer limited exposure and risk scarring.

An asymptomatic dome-shaped antral soft-tissue shadow over a tooth root is typically a mucous retention pseudocyst of the maxillary sinus. These require no treatment beyond observation when the associated tooth is sound and asymptomatic.
MRSA spreads primarily by direct contact with colonized skin, wounds, or contaminated hands and fomites — standard contact precautions. It is not transmitted by the airborne route like measles or TB.

Influenza is transmitted primarily via respiratory droplets, not direct contact. The options are poorly formed and none accurately capture the primary droplet/airborne mode. Among the listed distractors, direct contact is the least incorrect available choice.
A 16-year-old seeking abortion-related care is outside routine dental scope. Ethical and appropriate action is timely referral to a suitable medical facility or service.
Prophy cups are single-use disposable devices. After use on a patient they are discarded — they are not cleaned or sterilized for reuse on another patient.

Burning tongue sensation with a dry-mouth complaint but clinically moist mucosa is classic burning mouth syndrome — a neuropathic dysesthesia. It is not primary xerostomia from salivary hypofunction, where mucosa would appear dry.

Cervical enamel projections into the furcation promote plaque retention and attachment loss. Management involves odontoplasty/removal of the enamel projection combined with periodontal therapy to restore furcation health.

Bleeding on probing reflects the severity of gingival inflammation — specifically ulcerated pocket epithelium and increased vascularity. It is not primarily determined by the anatomic location of inflammation.

Third-trimester irreversible pulpitis requires definitive pulp therapy. Perform RCT with essential diagnostic and working radiographs using proper shielding rather than delaying treatment until after delivery, which risks pain, infection spread, and systemic complications.
Piezoelectric ultrasonic scalers vibrate in a primarily linear back-and-forth pattern along the tip axis. Magnetostrictive units have a more elliptical or orbital tip motion.

Digoxin has a narrow therapeutic index and its toxicity/adverse effects include gastrointestinal symptoms, nausea, and increased gag reflex. Among the listed cardiac medications, digoxin is the agent classically linked to gag and nausea complaints.

Primate spaces (mesial to maxillary canines, distal to mandibular canines) are developmental spacing that helps accommodate larger permanent incisors during the mixed dentition transition and resolves mild crowding.

With progressive attachment loss, recession, or aging, interdental papillae may flatten and disappear, creating black triangles. This loss of papillary form is a characteristic sign of established periodontal destruction.

Shallow superficial tongue lacerations typically heal well by secondary intention without the need for layered closure. Deep through-and-through lacerations crossing the muscle require layered suturing.

Conventional GIC requires protection during initial maturation. Definitive polishing should be delayed approximately 24 hours. Among the poorly formed options, after 3-5 min is the only time-based choice, though board teaching prefers next-day polishing after protecting with varnish or unfilled resin.

Uncontrolled diabetes predisposes to severe polymicrobial soft-tissue infections with rapid fascial spread. Necrotizing fasciitis is a life-threatening process that can present with limb necrosis and gangrene in diabetic patients.
